model.label: Gemini 3 Flash
model.description: Google • Fast Gemini model optimized for low-latency reasoning, multimodal inputs, and tool use.

pick.rationale: Michigan State enters this matchup with a superior analytical profile and the significant advantage of a healthy, balanced roster led by the tournament-tested Tom Izzo. While Louisville boasts a top-20 efficiency rating, the continued uncertainty surrounding star guard Mikel Brown Jr., who has missed several weeks with a back injury, severely limits their offensive ceiling. Expect the Spartans' elite defensive discipline and physical interior presence to stifle a shorthanded Louisville squad and secure a spot in the Sweet 16.

reasoningStep.title: Michigan State vs. Louisville Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Michigan State is favored due to their superior seeding, top-15 analytical ranking, and the significant injury concern for Louisville's star guard Mikel Brown Jr.
evidence: Louisville's Mikel Brown Jr. has missed nearly three weeks with a back injury and remains day-to-day.
Michigan State is a top-15 team in advanced metrics.
Tom Izzo has a storied history of success in the Round of 32.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

Tournament year: 2026
Current game id: r32-east-3
Current game label: East Round of 32 3
Round: Round of 32
Resolved teams: Louisville (louisville) vs Michigan State (michigan-state)
Prior committed picks: 38

result.title: Is Mikel Brown Jr. Playing? Latest on Louisville Star's Injury Status ...
result.snippet: Mikel Brown Jr. hasn't played a basketball game in nearly three weeks. Louisville's star freshman guard, a projected lottery pick who tied Wes Unseld's program scoring record with 45 points against NC State last month, has been reduced to watching his teammates practice while nursing the back injury that's defined his first college season.

result.title: Louisville basketball can blame Mikel Brown Jr.'s injury for March ...
result.snippet: Louisville basketball, a No. 6 seed, will face USF in the NCAA Tournament, but is left to wonder where it'd be had Mikel Brown Jr. stayed healthy.

result.title: Mikel Brown Jr. working on rehab with back injury in hopes of playing ...
result.snippet: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Mikel Brown Jr. is focusing on rehabbing the back injury that is keeping him out of the Atlantic Coast Conference Tournament. Louisville's star freshman and high-end NBA prospect is hoping the time off will have him ready for March Madness. "Yeah, it's hard," Brown said while sitting at his locker after missing the 24th-ranked Cardinals' second- round victory ...
